steps.title: Identify the greatest common factor
steps.content:
In the expression 5x + 15, find a common factor of both terms:
  • 5x = 5 · x
  • 15 = 5 · 3
The greatest common factor (GCF) is 5.

steps.title: Factor out the GCF
steps.content:
Divide each term by 5 and write the result inside parentheses:
5x + 15 = 5(x + 3)

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A: 5(x + 3) — distributing gives 5x + 15 ✓. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • Choice B: 5(x + 10) — distributing gives 5x + 50 ≠ 5x + 15. Eliminated.
  • Choice C: 5(x + 15) — distributing gives 5x + 75 ≠ 5x + 15. Eliminated.
  • Choice D: 5(x + 20) — distributing gives 5x + 100 ≠ 5x + 15. Eliminated.
That matches choice A — A is correct.
